agents.name: Coding Pass 1
agents.taskPrompt:
Make the largest coherent, reviewable change that materially advances the scoped outcome, preserve existing conventions, prioritize correctness, run relevant checks, and explain changed files and verification results.
Scoped goal from the goal agent: Build one coherent local-first question-bank/company-question evidence batch, avoiding rework of already evid…
Implement a reviewable question-bank integration pass that turns unmet/unknown source-aware practice-question checks into direct repo evidence. Focus on typed local data, validation, and UI discoverability: add or extend a structured question bank with original/paraphrased/company-associated questions, rich metadata, source notes, tags, difficulty, estimated time, round type, role relevance, firm associations, hints, and step-by-step explanations. Add a searchable/filterable Question Bank view and ensure company pages surface a dedicated firm-associated question section using the same local data. Preserve the existing React/TypeScript/Vite stack, routing style, local data architecture, and test conventions. Do not spend this pass re-proving the already evidenced mental math speed game, recruiting roadmap, or generic company page sections except where linking company pages to firm-associated questions is necessary. Do not add runtime APIs, credentials, scraping, copied proprietary content, or paywalled data. Done means the app has deterministic local question data, validation coverage, UI evidence for global search/filter and company-linked questions, and passing targeted tests/build. Keep the work scoped to one coherent coding pass. Prefer the largest reviewable batch of related implementation, tests, and evidence that fits the approved recommendation. If the recommendation still feels broad, narrow it to the strongest cohesive batch rather than a tiny evidence-only slice. Do not start unrelated follow-up work.
Keep the work scoped to one coherent coding pass. Prefer the largest reviewable batch of related implementation, tests, and evidence that fits the approved recommendation. If the recommendation still feels broad, narrow it to the strongest cohesive batch rather than a tiny evidence-only slice. Do not start unrelated follow-up work.
Acceptance criteria:
- A typed local question content model distinguishes original, paraphrased public-report, company-reported, Green Book-style, coding, user-created, and recruiting/compensation note categories where relevant.
- The seed question bank includes a meaningful batch of original or clearly paraphrased quant-style questions across probability, expected value, brain teasers, market making/trading, coding/data, and ML/statistics, each with hints and step-by-step explanations.
- Each question includes searchable metadata: tags, topic, difficulty, estimated time, round type, role relevance, firm associations when applicable, source notes/source type, and uncertainty/confidence where applicable.
- The UI exposes a Question Bank experience with search and filters for topic, difficulty, role, firm, and source/category, with clear links into practice or review detail states.
- Major company pages display a dedicated firm-associated questions section populated from the shared question bank rather than duplicated hardcoded content.
- Validation/unit tests and at least one smoke test prove the data model, metadata completeness, search/filter behavior, and company-question rendering from local fixtures.
Integrity will verify with:
- Run targeted unit tests for question validation/content completeness and search/filter logic.
- Run or add a smoke test that opens the Question Bank, exercises filters/search, and verifies a question detail/explanation is visible.
- Run or add a smoke test that visits at least one major company page and verifies firm-associated questions render from shared data.
- Run the project build and the relevant existing test script(s) to ensure TypeScript and routing remain intact.

{"type":"agentMessage","id":"msg_088461ca3a8d0996016a0354d4249c8191ad289a8b7bed2fb1","text":"Implemented a coherent local-first Question Bank integration pass.

- `src/lib/questionBankTypes.ts` — added the structured question content model.
- `src/lib/questionBankSearch.ts` — added deterministic search/filter/facet logic.
- `src/lib/questionBankValidation.ts` — added metadata/completeness/provenance validation.

- `src/lib/masteryStorage.ts` — added `question-bank-item` mastery support.
- `src/styles/global.css` — added Question Bank/filter/detail styling.
- Tests:

 - `src/lib/questionBankSearch.test.ts`
 - updated `src/lib/masteryStorage.test.ts`
 - `smoke/question-bank.spec.ts`

- `npm run build` ✅ passed

agents.name: Goal Agent
agents.taskPrompt:
Turn approved recommendations into scoped, testable execution briefs. Keep plans concrete, bounded, aligned with the outcome strategy, and focused on evidence that moves the Ultimate Goal checklist forward.
Turn the approved recommendation into a scoped goal for the next coding pass.
The output must represent one bounded task for a single cycle, not a broad multi-phase project.
The result must be executable by one coding agent in one pass. If related checks share implementation paths, tests, or evidence, plan the largest coherent reviewable batch instead of narrowing to a tiny evidence-only slice.
Do not scope another coding pass just to re-prove a semantically identical checklist batch that already has direct evidence. If the approved recommendation is about checklist cleanup, focus the brief on the concrete missing evidence or redundant checks named in the recommendation.
`executionBrief` should be a full prompt for the coding agent: what to change, where to focus, what to avoid, and how to know the task is done.
Make acceptanceCriteria concrete and testable.
Keep acceptanceCriteria to at most 6 bullets and testStrategy to at most 4 focused checks.
Use the Goal checklist as the completion source of truth. Prefer a scoped plan that turns a coherent group of unmet or unknown required checks into met checks with evidence.
Keep constraints aligned with the Ultimate Goal and the repository boundaries.
Use the outcome strategy below to keep the scoped plan pointed at the best finished project outcome.
Outcome strategy:
- Ultimate target: Build quant_interview_prep into a local-first, professional-grade interview preparation website for quant research, quant trading, and machine learning recruiting that helps me practice until answers, frameworks, and problem-solving patterns become automatic before senior-year internship or full-time interviews.
- Completion source of truth: 50/122 required goal checks met (41%).
- Primary next move: Satisfy the highest-impact coherent batch of related goal checks with direct repository evidence.
- Highest-impact open checks: Each company page includes a “How to Prepare” section with recommended platform drills, topics to review, common mistakes, and a suggested 1-week and…; Each company page includes a company overview, business model summary, role types, office locations where relevant, recruiting timeline, interview pr…; Each company page includes a compensation section for internship and full-time roles, with approximate ranges, year/date context, location notes, sou…; Each company page includes a dedicated question section showing firm-associated questions, reported question categories, similar original practice qu….
- Efficient batching opportunity: includes: The app includes a mental math speed game with arithmetic, fractions, percentages, logs/approximations, probability arithmetic, expected value arithm…; The app includes a recruiting roadmap that organizes preparation by week, topic, difficulty, and target firm, with recommended sequences for someone…; The app includes individual company pages for major quant firms including Jane Street, Citadel Securities, Citadel, Two Sigma, D. E. Shaw, Optiver, S…; The app includes original practice questions created specifically for the platform, with realistic quant-interview style wording and detailed explana….
- Current blockers: none.
- Quality bar: The final project should feel like a polished, professional quant recruiting preparation terminal: fast, structured, rigorous, source-aware, visually appealing, and genuinely useful for daily practice. Core functionalit…
- Preserve constraints: If an existing project stack is already present, preserve the existing stack, conventions, scripts, routing, styling system, and architecture unless…; If this is a greenfield project, prefer a local-first React, TypeScript, and Vite-style web app with structured local data files and local persistenc…; The website must be able t [truncated from 16017 characters]
agents.lastMessageSnippet: Summary: Build one coherent local-first question-bank/company-question evidence batch, avoiding rework of already evidenced mental-math, roadmap, and company-guide shell checks.

agents.name: Recommendation Agent
agents.taskPrompt:
Produce ranked next actions based on the repository state, the current cycle, unresolved issues, and the highest-impact unmet Ultimate Goal checks. Perform checklist governance first: add only necessary required checks, remove redundant or no-longer-applicable checks with evidence, and prefer coherent one-cycle batches that improve the finished project outcome with direct evidence.
Inspect the active project and recommend the next bounded tasks for the workflow.
First decide whether the Ultimate Goal is already satisfied strongly enough that the workflow should stop opening new cycles. If it is, mark the goal as satisfied and return zero recommendations.
Return 0 to 5 recommendations.
Output terse JSON only. No greetings, markdown, filler, or restating unchanged logs/checklists.
Plan like a small SWE team. Checklist items are acceptance checks; a cycle is the next coherent task group based on shared paths, tests, evidence, user value, and blocking order.
Recommendations must be concrete single-cycle tasks. Use medium scope for cohesive batches, small only for isolated checks, and split unrelated or umbrella work.
If required checks are unmet/unknown, rank the next coherent required-check group ahead of generic stabilization unless a real blocker exists. Do not claim met without repository evidence or validation output.
For live-data, trading, brokerage, market-data, analytics, or finance interfaces, prefer a provider abstraction with offline demo/mock mode, credentialed live adapter mode, and loading/error/empty states. Paid credentials require operator choice.
Checklist governance: return goalCheckUpdates only for changed items. Add required checks only when indispensable, consolidate duplicates, mark redundant/non-goal/user-derived removals as not_applicable, and keep evidence concise.
Completion: percentComplete is met required checks divided by total required checks. goal_satisfied requires every required check met and no open blocker.
If the Ultimate Goal is satisfied, set the goal-completion assessment to goal_satisfied and leave the recommendations array empty.
Use only relative project paths in relatedPaths.
Use the deterministic outcome strategy below as the decision frame. It is guidance, not a license to ignore concrete repository evidence.
Outcome strategy:
- Ultimate target: Build quant_interview_prep into a local-first, professional-grade interview preparation website for quant research, quant trading, and machine learning recruiting that helps me practice until answers, frameworks, and problem-solving patterns become automatic before senior-year internship or full-time interviews.
- Completion source of truth: 42/114 required goal checks met (37%).
- Primary next move: Satisfy the highest-impact coherent batch of related goal checks with direct repository evidence.

- Efficient batching opportunity: includes: The app includes a mental math speed game with arithmetic, fractions, percentages, logs/approximations, probability arithmetic, expected value arithm…; The app includes a recruiting roadmap that organizes preparation by week, topic, difficulty, and target firm, with recommended sequences for someone… [truncated from 20828 characters]

agents.name: Recommendation Agent
agents.taskPrompt:
Produce ranked next actions based on the repository state, the current cycle, unresolved issues, and the highest-impact unmet Ultimate Goal checks. Perform checklist governance first: add only necessary required checks, remove redundant or no-longer-applicable checks with evidence, and prefer coherent one-cycle batches that improve the finished project outcome with direct evidence.
Inspect the active project and recommend the next bounded tasks for the workflow.
First decide whether the Ultimate Goal is already satisfied strongly enough that the workflow should stop opening new cycles. If it is, mark the goal as satisfied and return zero recommendations.
Return 0 to 5 recommendations.
Every recommendation must be a concrete, single-cycle task. Prefer the largest coherent reviewable batch when related Goal checks share implementation paths, tests, or evidence; split unrelated work apart.
Think like a small SWE team decomposing the Ultimate Goal into tasks. Checklist items are acceptance requirements; a cycle is a task that should cover the coherent subset of requirements that naturally belong together.
Prefer recommendations that are easy for a coding agent to execute and for an integrity agent to verify in one cycle, even when the recommendation covers a small batch of related checks.
When any required Goal checklist item is unmet or unknown, rank direct work on those checklist items ahead of generic stabilization, package-script cleanup, operator-feedback tweaks, or recently changed file follow-up unless there is an explicit open blocker.
For checklist work, recommend the option that can most directly move one coherent group of related required checks from unmet/unknown to met with repository evidence. Avoid claiming a feature check is satisfied through unrelated harness or package-only work.
Use the task map to reason about how many cycles the project probably needs. Do not choose a random fixed number of checklist items; choose the next logical task group based on shared implementation paths, tests, evidence, user value, and blocking order.
Default to medium scope for cohesive task groups, and small scope only for a genuinely isolated single-check task. Large scope should still be split into the next reviewable task group instead of flattened into item-by-item churn.
Do not recommend end-to-end milestones, phase-wide deliverables, or umbrella workflows that mix unrelated areas.
Do not plan to complete checklist items one by one when a coherent implementation or validation batch can cover related items. State the batch as one recommendation with the target checks named in the summary or rationale.
When a generated or improved interface needs live external data, recommend a provider abstraction with explicit offline demo/mock mode, live adapter mode when credentials are configured, and missing-credentials loading/error/empty states. Do not silently replace needed live data with local-only mock data.
For trading, brokerage, market-data, analytics, or finance interfaces, do not hardcode one provider unless the user chose it. If a free/no-card API key would unlock the live feature, recommend implementing the adapter and creating a credential request instead of settling for mock-only behavior; paid credentials require explicit operator choice.
Before ranking implementation work, perform a Goal checklist governance pass. Decide whether the current checklist is complete, missing an absolutely necessary required check, or carrying redundant/unnecessary checks that should no longer block completion.
Use goalCheckUpdates to keep the checklist adaptive: add missing required checks, update evidence/status, remove redundant checks, and consolidate near-duplicates. Prefer meaningful governance over artificial item limits.
If the current checklist is already large, do not add new required checks unless they are indispensable safety/security constraints. Consolidate redundant checks or mark no-longer-applic [truncated from 24873 characters]

You are coding one bounded pass in quant_interview_prep. Focus on the existing company-guide implementation, not on re-proving mental math or roadmap checks that already have direct evidence. First inspect the current data model, validation, routes/components, and tests for company pages. Then update the smallest coherent set of files needed so every existing major quant firm page renders complete structured sections: company overview/business model, role types/offices/recruiting timeline/interview process, How to Prepare, compensation, and dedicated questions. Likely focus paths include src/data/company* or equivalent, src/lib/companyValidation*, the company page UI in src/App.tsx or routed components, and smoke/company-pages.spec.ts. Add or tighten typed validation so missing required company-page sections fail deterministic tests. Keep the app fully local-first: store structured local content and source notes in repo data files; do not add runtime scraping, paid APIs, credentials, auth, deployment, or external service dependencies. Use public, paraphrased, source-aware summaries; mark uncertainty explicitly for compensation or interview reports. For questions, add firm-associated categories plus similar original practice questions with explanations and source notes rather than copied interview-bank text. The pass is done when company validation and smoke evidence directly demonstrate that the highest-impact company-page checklist checks are satisfied without regressing existing build or smoke tests. Keep the work scoped to one coherent coding pass. Prefer the largest reviewable batch of related implementation, tests, and evidence that fits the approved recommendation. - Each company page includes compensation entries for internship and full-time roles where available, with approximate range, year/date context, location, role, source type, and uncertainty note; unavailable items must display an explicit researched-gap note rather than silent blanks.
- Each company page includes a dedicated question section with firm-associated categories plus similar original practice questions, difficulty/tags, answer or explanation, and source/provenance notes.
- Validation tests fail on missing required company-page subsections or missing compensation/source context, and pass with the updated local data.
- The updated UI remains local-first and passes the targeted company-page smoke test plus the production build.
Integrity will verify with:
- Run the targeted company validation unit tests, adding/updating them to assert required sections, compensation context, question provenance, and How-to-Prepare completeness.

agents.name: Goal Agent
agents.taskPrompt:
Turn approved recommendations into scoped, testable execution briefs. Keep plans concrete, bounded, aligned with the outcome strategy, and focused on evidence that moves the Ultimate Goal checklist forward.
Turn the approved recommendation into a scoped goal for the next coding pass.
The output must represent one bounded task for a single cycle, not a broad multi-phase project.
The result must be executable by one coding agent in one pass. If related checks share implementation paths, tests, or evidence, plan the largest coherent reviewable batch instead of narrowing to a tiny evidence-only slice.
Do not scope another coding pass just to re-prove a semantically identical checklist batch that already has direct evidence. If the approved recommendation is about checklist cleanup, focus the brief on the concrete missing evidence or redundant checks named in the recommendation.
`executionBrief` should be a full prompt for the coding agent: what to change, where to focus, what to avoid, and how to know the task is done.
Make acceptanceCriteria concrete and testable.
Keep acceptanceCriteria to at most 6 bullets and testStrategy to at most 4 focused checks.
Use the Goal checklist as the completion source of truth. Prefer a scoped plan that turns a coherent group of unmet or unknown required checks into met checks with evidence.
Keep constraints aligned with the Ultimate Goal and the repository boundaries.
Use the outcome strategy below to keep the scoped plan pointed at the best finished project outcome.

agents.name: Recommendation Agent
agents.taskPrompt:
Produce ranked next actions based on the repository state, the current cycle, unresolved issues, and the highest-impact unmet Ultimate Goal checks. Perform checklist governance first: add only necessary required checks, remove redundant or no-longer-applicable checks with evidence, and prefer coherent one-cycle batches that improve the finished project outcome with direct evidence.
Inspect the active project and recommend the next bounded tasks for the workflow.
First decide whether the Ultimate Goal is already satisfied strongly enough that the workflow should stop opening new cycles. If it is, mark the goal as satisfied and return zero recommendations.
Return 0 to 5 recommendations.
Every recommendation must be a concrete, single-cycle task. Prefer the largest coherent reviewable batch when related Goal checks share implementation paths, tests, or evidence; split unrelated work apart.
Think like a small SWE team decomposing the Ultimate Goal into tasks. Checklist items are acceptance requirements; a cycle is a task that should cover the coherent subset of requirements that naturally belong together.
Prefer recommendations that are easy for a coding agent to execute and for an integrity agent to verify in one cycle, even when the recommendation covers a small batch of related checks.
When any required Goal checklist item is unmet or unknown, rank direct work on those checklist items ahead of generic stabilization, package-script cleanup, operator-feedback tweaks, or recently changed file follow-up unless there is an explicit open blocker.
For checklist work, recommend the option that can most directly move one coherent group of related required checks from unmet/unknown to met with repository evidence. Avoid claiming a feature check is satisfied through unrelated harness or package-only work.
Use the task map to reason about how many cycles the project probably needs. Do not choose a random fixed number of checklist items; choose the next logical task group based on shared implementation paths, tests, evidence, user value, and blocking order.
Default to medium scope for cohesive task groups, and small scope only for a genuinely isolated single-check task. Large scope should still be split into the next reviewable task group instead of flattened into item-by-item churn.
Do not recommend end-to-end milestones, phase-wide deliverables, or umbrella workflows that mix unrelated areas.
Do not plan to complete checklist items one by one when a coherent implementation or validation batch can cover related items. State the batch as one recommendation with the target checks named in the summary or rationale.
When a generated or improved interface needs live external data, recommend a provider abstraction with explicit offline demo/mock mode, live adapter mode when credentials are configured, and missing-credentials loading/error/empty states. Do not silently replace needed live data with local-only mock data.
For trading, brokerage, market-data, analytics, or finance interfaces, do not hardcode one provider unless the user chose it. If a free/no-card API key would unlock the live feature, recommend implementing the adapter and creating a credential request instead of settling for mock-only behavior; paid credentials require explicit operator choice.
Before ranking implementation work, perform a Goal checklist governance pass. Decide whether the current checklist is complete, missing an absolutely necessary required check, or carrying redundant/unnecessary checks that should no longer block completion.
Use goalCheckUpdates to keep the checklist adaptive: add missing required checks, update evidence/status, remove redundant checks, and consolidate near-duplicates. Prefer meaningful governance over artificial item limits.
If the current checklist is already large, do not add new required checks unless they are indispensable safety/security constraints. Consolidate redundant checks or mark no-longer-applic [truncated from 24171 characters]

Build one coherent local-first mastery-controls pass. Add typed per-practice-item mastery state covering the required controls: new, learning, needs review, difficult, mastered, and favorite. Focus on current local practice items: mini-game prompts and company-page original practice questions. Prefer a small dedicated storage/helper module or an extension of src/lib/progressStorage.ts with deterministic defaults, malformed-storage recovery, and no external dependencies. Wire controls into src/App.tsx where users encounter prompts/questions: current mini-game prompt feedback or prompt chrome, review queue cards, and company Practice Questions cards. Persist choices in localStorage, show readable labels, and make dashboard/review surfaces reflect weak/mastered/favorite/needs-review state. Avoid rebuilding the game engine, adding new content batches, changing routing architecture, or adding APIs/accounts/cloud sync. Done means the UI visibly lets a user mark practice items as new/learning/needs review/difficult/mastered/favorite, those choices survive reload, and targeted unit plus smoke evidence proves it. Keep the work scoped to one coherent coding pass. Prefer the largest reviewable batch of related implementation, tests, and evidence that fits the approved recommendation. Acceptance criteria:
- A typed mastery model exists with statuses new, learning, needs-review, difficult, mastered plus favorite, keyed by stable local practice item IDs for both mini-game prompts and company practice questions.
- Mastery controls are visible on mini-game practice and company Practice Questions cards, with accessible labels and persisted localStorage updates.
- Dashboard or review queue visibly uses mastery state to surface weak/needs-review/difficult items and mastered/favorite items without breaking existing attempt history.
- Malformed or missing stored mastery data falls back deterministically to new/non-favorite and is covered by tests.
- No runtime API, credentials, auth, scraping, deployment, or paid service dependency is added.
- npm run test, npm run build, and targeted smoke covering mastery persistence pass.
Integrity will verify with:
- Add or update unit tests for mastery storage/defaulting, status transitions, favorite toggles, and malformed localStorage recovery.
- Add or update review/dashboard logic tests proving needs-review/difficult/mastered/favorite state affects displayed/prioritized items.
- Update smoke/mini-games.spec.ts to mark a prompt, reload, and verify the persisted mastery label/favorite state.
- If company question controls are not covered by the mini-game smoke, add a focused company-page smoke assertion for marking one practice question.
Ultimate Goal: Build quant_interview_prep into a local-first, professional-grade interview preparation website for quant research, quant trading, and machine learning recruiting that helps me practice until answers, frameworks, and problem-solving patterns become automatic before senior-year internship or full-time interviews.

Default to medium scope for cohesive task groups, and small scope only for a genuinely isolated single-check task. Large scope should still be split into the next reviewable task group instead of flattened into item-by-item churn.

Do not silently replace needed live data with local-only mock data.
For trading, brokerage, market-data, analytics, or finance interfaces, do not hardcode one provider unless the user chose it. If a free/no-card API key would unlock the live feature, recommend implementing the adapter and creating a credential request instead of settling for mock-only behavior; paid credentials require explicit operator choice.

For checklist work, recommend the option that can most directly move one coherent group of related required checks from unmet/unknown to met with repository evidence. Avoid claiming a feature check is satisfied through unrelated harness or package-only work.
Default to medium scope for cohesive batches of 2-4 related checks, and small scope for a true single-check task. Large scope should still be avoided.
Do not recommend end-to-end milestones, phase-wide deliverables, or umbrella workflows that mix unrelated areas.
When a generated or improved interface needs live external data, recommend a provider abstraction with explicit offline demo/mock mode, live adapter mode when credentials are configured, and missing-credentials loading/error/empty states. Do not silently replace needed live data with local-only mock data.
For trading, brokerage, market-data, analytics, or finance interfaces, do not hardcode one provider unless the user chose it. If a free/no-card API key would unlock the live feature, recommend implementing the adapter and creating a credential request instead of settling for mock-only behavior; paid credentials require explicit operator choice.
Before ranking implementation work, perform a Goal checklist governance pass. Decide whether the current checklist is complete, missing an absolutely necessary required check, or carrying redundant/unnecessary checks that should no longer block completion.
Add a new required checklist item only when it is indispensable to the stated Ultimate Goal or an explicit safety/security constraint and is not already covered by an existing check. Do not add speculative nice-to-have work as required.
When two or more checklist items describe the same obligation, update the strongest existing item and include remove updates for redundant duplicates. For user-derived checks, remove means mark not_applicable with evidence rather than silently deleting the operator's intent.
If repository evidence shows a checklist item is unnecessary, impossible under the stated non-goals, or redundant with a more precise check, return a goalCheckUpdates entry with action remove, status not_applicable, and concrete rationale.
Maintain the Goal checklist. You may add a required check, mark a check unmet/unknown if evidence shows it is not actually done, mark a check met only when repository evidence or validation output supports it, or remove/not-applicable checks when they are redundant or no longer necessary.
Do not mark the Ultimate Goal satisfied unless every required Goal checklist item is met and no open blockers remain. Accepted decisions, comp [truncated from 13984 characters]
